speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: How far is it, my lord, to Berkeley now?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Believe me, noble lord,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: I am a stranger here in Gloucestershire.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: These high wild hills and rough uneven ways

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Draws out our miles and makes them wearisome.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And yet your fair discourse hath been as sugar,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Making the hard way sweet and delectable.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But I bethink me what a weary way

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: From Ravenspurgh to Cotshall will be found

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: In Ross and Willoughby, wanting your company,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Which, I protest, hath very much beguiled

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The tediousness and process of my travel.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But theirs is sweetened with the hope to have

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The present benefit which I possess,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And hope to joy is little less in joy

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Than hope enjoyed. By this the weary lords

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Shall make their way seem short as mine hath done

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: By sight of what I have, your noble company.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Of much less value is my company

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Than your good words. But who comes here?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: It is my son, young Harry Percy,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Sent from my brother Worcester whencesoever.—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Harry, how fares your uncle?

speaker: PERCY
text: I had thought, my lord, to have learned his health of you.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Why, is he not with the Queen?

speaker: PERCY
text: No, my good lord, he hath forsook the court,

speaker: PERCY
text: Broken his staff of office, and dispersed

speaker: PERCY
text: The Household of the King.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: What was his reason? He was not so resolved

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: When last we spake together.

speaker: PERCY
text: Because your Lordship was proclaimèd traitor.

speaker: PERCY
text: But he, my lord, is gone to Ravenspurgh

speaker: PERCY
text: To offer service to the Duke of Hereford,

speaker: PERCY
text: And sent me over by Berkeley to discover

speaker: PERCY
text: What power the Duke of York had levied there,

speaker: PERCY
text: Then with directions to repair to Ravenspurgh.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Have you forgot the Duke of Hereford, boy?

speaker: PERCY
text: No, my good lord, for that is not forgot

speaker: PERCY
text: Which ne’er I did remember. To my knowledge

speaker: PERCY
text: I never in my life did look on him.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Then learn to know him now. This is the Duke.

speaker: PERCY
text: My gracious lord, I tender you my service,

speaker: PERCY
text: Such as it is, being tender, raw, and young,

speaker: PERCY
text: Which elder days shall ripen and confirm

speaker: PERCY
text: To more approvèd service and desert.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I thank thee, gentle Percy, and be sure

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I count myself in nothing else so happy

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As in a soul rememb’ring my good friends;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And as my fortune ripens with thy love,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: It shall be still thy true love’s recompense.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My heart this covenant makes, my hand thus seals it.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: How far is it to Berkeley, and what stir

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Keeps good old York there with his men of war?

speaker: PERCY
text: There stands the castle by yon tuft of trees,

speaker: PERCY
text: Manned with three hundred men, as I have heard,

speaker: PERCY
text: And in it are the Lords of York, Berkeley, and Seymour,

speaker: PERCY
text: None else of name and noble estimate.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Here come the Lords of Ross and Willoughby,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Bloody with spurring, fiery red with haste.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Welcome, my lords. I wot your love pursues

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: A banished traitor. All my treasury

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Is yet but unfelt thanks, which, more enriched,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Shall be your love and labor’s recompense.

speaker: ROSS
text: Your presence makes us rich, most noble lord.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: And far surmounts our labor to attain it.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Evermore thank’s the exchequer of the poor,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Which, till my infant fortune comes to years,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Stands for my bounty. But who comes here?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: It is my Lord of Berkeley, as I guess.

speaker: BERKELEY
text: My Lord of Hereford, my message is to you.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My lord, my answer is—to “Lancaster”;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And I am come to seek that name in England.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And I must find that title in your tongue

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Before I make reply to aught you say.

speaker: BERKELEY
text: Mistake me not, my lord, ’tis not my meaning

speaker: BERKELEY
text: To rase one title of your honor out.

speaker: BERKELEY
text: To you, my lord, I come, what lord you will,

speaker: BERKELEY
text: From the most gracious regent of this land,

speaker: BERKELEY
text: The Duke of York, to know what pricks you on

speaker: BERKELEY
text: To take advantage of the absent time,

speaker: BERKELEY
text: And fright our native peace with self-borne arms.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I shall not need transport my words by you.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Here comes his Grace in person.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My noble uncle.

speaker: YORK
text: Show me thy humble heart and not thy knee,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose duty is deceivable and false.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My gracious uncle—

speaker: YORK
text: Tut, tut!

speaker: YORK
text: Grace me no grace, nor uncle me no uncle.

speaker: YORK
text: I am no traitor’s uncle, and that word “grace”

speaker: YORK
text: In an ungracious mouth is but profane.

speaker: YORK
text: Why have those banished and forbidden legs

speaker: YORK
text: Dared once to touch a dust of England’s ground?

speaker: YORK
text: But then, more why: why have they dared to march

speaker: YORK
text: So many miles upon her peaceful bosom,

speaker: YORK
text: Frighting her pale-faced villages with war

speaker: YORK
text: And ostentation of despisèd arms?

speaker: YORK
text: Com’st thou because the anointed king is hence?

speaker: YORK
text: Why, foolish boy, the King is left behind

speaker: YORK
text: And in my loyal bosom lies his power.

speaker: YORK
text: Were I but now lord of such hot youth

speaker: YORK
text: As when brave Gaunt thy father and myself

speaker: YORK
text: Rescued the Black Prince, that young Mars of men,

speaker: YORK
text: From forth the ranks of many thousand French,

speaker: YORK
text: O, then, how quickly should this arm of mine,

speaker: YORK
text: Now prisoner to the palsy, chastise thee

speaker: YORK
text: And minister correction to thy fault!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My gracious uncle, let me know my fault.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: On what condition stands it and wherein?

speaker: YORK
text: Even in condition of the worst degree,

speaker: YORK
text: In gross rebellion and detested treason.

speaker: YORK
text: Thou art a banished man and here art come,

speaker: YORK
text: Before the expiration of thy time,

speaker: YORK
text: In braving arms against thy sovereign.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As I was banished, I was banished Hereford,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: But as I come, I come for Lancaster.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And, noble uncle, I beseech your Grace

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Look on my wrongs with an indifferent eye.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: You are my father, for methinks in you

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I see old Gaunt alive. O, then, my father,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Will you permit that I shall stand condemned

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: A wandering vagabond, my rights and royalties

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Plucked from my arms perforce and given away

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To upstart unthrifts? Wherefore was I born?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: If that my cousin king be king in England,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: It must be granted I am Duke of Lancaster.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: You have a son, Aumerle, my noble cousin.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Had you first died and he been thus trod down,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: He should have found his uncle Gaunt a father

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To rouse his wrongs and chase them to the bay.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I am denied to sue my livery here,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And yet my letters patents give me leave.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My father’s goods are all distrained and sold,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And these, and all, are all amiss employed.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What would you have me do? I am a subject,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And I challenge law. Attorneys are denied me,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And therefore personally I lay my claim

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To my inheritance of free descent.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The noble duke hath been too much abused.

speaker: ROSS
text: It stands your Grace upon to do him right.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Base men by his endowments are made great.

speaker: YORK
text: My lords of England, let me tell you this:

speaker: YORK
text: I have had feeling of my cousin’s wrongs

speaker: YORK
text: And labored all I could to do him right.

speaker: YORK
text: But in this kind to come, in braving arms,

speaker: YORK
text: Be his own carver and cut out his way

speaker: YORK
text: To find out right with wrong, it may not be.

speaker: YORK
text: And you that do abet him in this kind

speaker: YORK
text: Cherish rebellion and are rebels all.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The noble duke hath sworn his coming is

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But for his own, and for the right of that

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: We all have strongly sworn to give him aid.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And let him never see joy that breaks that oath.

speaker: YORK
text: Well, well. I see the issue of these arms.

speaker: YORK
text: I cannot mend it, I must needs confess,

speaker: YORK
text: Because my power is weak and all ill-left.

speaker: YORK
text: But if I could, by Him that gave me life,

speaker: YORK
text: I would attach you all and make you stoop

speaker: YORK
text: Unto the sovereign mercy of the King.

speaker: YORK
text: But since I cannot, be it known unto you

speaker: YORK
text: I do remain as neuter. So fare you well—

speaker: YORK
text: Unless you please to enter in the castle

speaker: YORK
text: And there repose you for this night.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: An offer, uncle, that we will accept.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: But we must win your Grace to go with us

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To Bristow Castle, which they say is held

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By Bushy, Bagot, and their complices,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The caterpillars of the commonwealth,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Which I have sworn to weed and pluck away.

speaker: YORK
text: It may be I will go with you; but yet I’ll pause,

speaker: YORK
text: For I am loath to break our country’s laws.

speaker: YORK
text: Nor friends nor foes, to me welcome you are.

speaker: YORK
text: Things past redress are now with me past care.
